item.title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Tehran retaliates
item.description: Drone attacks caused fire at Kuwait’s Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery on Friday, state media reported, as authorities in several Gulf states said they were responding to attacks from Iran....

item.title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Tehran retaliates
item.description: Drone attacks caused fire at Kuwait’s Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery on Friday, state media reported, as authorities in several Gulf states said they were responding to attacks from Iran....

item.title: Holidaymakers continue to leave Dhaka
item.description: Holidaymakers continued to leave Dhaka by buses, launches and trains on Friday, a day before Eid-ul-Fitr, to celebrate the festival in their ancestral homes with their near and dear ones......City & Local Guides

item.title: US, Japan announce $40 bn nuclear power project
item.description: The United States and Japan announced Thursday a $40 billion project to build nuclear reactors in Tennessee and Alabama, after a meeting of the two countries’ leaders in Washington....

item.title: Supply shortage of fuel oil across Bangladesh continues
item.description: People continued to suffer in long queues to refuel their vehicles at filling stations across the country, including the capital, on Friday as the supply shortage of the items from the state-owned Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation persisted......Local business directory

item.title: Meat, chicken, lemon, cucumber prices rise ahead of Eid
item.description: Prices of key protein items — beef, mutton and chicken — increased, on average, by Tk 50 per kilogram in kitchen markets in the capital on Friday, ahead of Eid-ul-Fitr......

item.title: Israeli arms producing company in Czech Republic set on fire
item.description: Czech police are investigating the fire at the arms company on Friday as a potential terrorist attack, the interior minister said, after an anti-Israel group claimed responsibility......

item.title: Rain likely across Bangladesh during Eid celebrations
item.description: Rain and thunder showers are likely disrupt Eid celebrations at places across Bangladesh on Saturday......Local business directory

item.title: Rain likely across Bangladesh on Eid day
item.description: Rain and thunder showers accompanied by temporary gusty or squally wind and lightning flashes are likely to occur at a few places at all eight divisions on Saturday, when Bangladesh will celebrate Eid-ul-Fitr......

item.title: BNP leader, another killed in lightning strikes in Faridpur
item.description: A local leader of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and a farmer were killed in separate lightning strikes while working in fields in Madhukhali of Faridpur on Friday......

item.title: Doha gymnastics World Cup event scrapped because of Middle East war
item.description: The gymnastics World Cup event scheduled to take place in Doha in April has been cancelled due to the war in the Middle East, the sport’s governing body announced on Friday......

item.title: Iran fires at Israel after Netanyahu boasts of war gains
item.description: Iran launched multiple rounds of missiles towards Israel late Thursday, after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu declared the Islamic republic has been ‘decimated’ by war....

item.title: Drone attack causes fire at Kuwait oil refinery
item.description: Drone attacks hit Kuwait’s Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery on Friday, causing several fires but no casualties, state media said, citing the national oil company......

item.title: Italy, Germany and France offer help with Hormuz only after ceasefire
item.description: Six major international powers said Thursday they were ready 'to contribute to' ensuring safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, though three stressed that any initiative would take place post-ceasefire.......

item.title: Fuel prices rise in India amid ME conflicts
item.description: Prices of premium quality petrol and industrial diesel rose in India by nearly 2.30 Indian rupees (nearly 2 US cents) per liter and 22 Indian rupees (23 US cents) per liter, respectively, on Friday, confirmed the oil marketing companies......

item.title: Chinese scientists uncover why pain gets worse at night
item.description: Many people with chronic pain find their discomfort manageable during the day but unbearable at night, and a team from China has now uncovered the neural mechanism behind these daily fluctuations.......

item.title: FIFA planning for World Cup to 'go ahead as scheduled' amid Iran uncertainty
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino on Thursday underlined the body's commitment to making sure the World Cup goes ahead 'as scheduled' with 'all teams' taking part amid doubts about Iran's presence at the tournament.......

item.title: Two stewards suspended for black-marketing train tickets
item.description: Two stewards of onboard service provider SA Corporation have been permanently dismissed for black-marketing tickets of the Dhaka-Chattogram-bound Subarna Express train......

item.title: Bangladesh, India discuss ways to advance bilateral ties
item.description: Bangladesh and India on Friday discussed bilateral cooperation between the two countries as both sides eye stronger people-centric relations in the future......Local business directory

item.title: Bangladesh national injured in BSF firing
item.description: A Bangladesh national sustained injuries after being hit by pellets fired by India’s Border Security Force along the Notunpara border area in Jibannagar upazila of Chuadanga on Thursday......

item.title: Trump calls NATO allies ‘cowards’ over Iran
item.description: US allies ‘don’t want to help open the Strait of Hormuz, a simple military maneuver that is the single reason for the high oil prices. So easy for them to do, with so little risk,’ he posted......

item.title: Trump calls NATO allies ‘cowards’ over Iran
item.description: US allies ‘don’t want to help open the Strait of Hormuz, a simple military maneuver that is the single reason for the high oil prices. So easy for them to do, with so little risk,’ he posted......

item.title: Bangladesh to celebrate Eid today
item.description: Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the biggest religious festivals of Muslims, is going to be celebrated across Bangladesh today as the moon of Shawwal of 1447 Hijri was sighted in the country’s sky on Friday evening......

item.title: Moon sighted, Bangladesh to celebrate Eid on Saturday
item.description: Eid-ul-Fitr will be celebrated across Bangladesh on Saturday as the Shawwal moon was sighted in the country’s sky on Friday evening......

item.title: PM Tarique Rahman for sharing joy of Eid with all
item.description: Bangladesh prime minister Tarique Rahman has called for sharing the happiness of the Eid-ul-Fitr with all, being inspired by the ideals of the Ramadan......

item.title: Two killed, 15 injured as bus hits auto-rickshaws in Mymensingh
item.description: Two people, including a woman, were killed and at least 15 others injured when a bus hit two auto-rickshaws in Bhaluka on Friday afternoon......

item.title: Padma, Jamuna bridges set new traffic, toll collection records
item.description: The country’s two major bridges, the Padma Bridge and the Jamuna Bridge, have recorded their highest figures for both vehicle crossings and toll collection during this year’s Eid-ul-Fitr travel rush......

item.title: 280 Bangladeshis evacuated from Iran to reach home early Saturday
item.description: Around 280 Bangladeshi nationals evacuated from Iran due to the ongoing war are expected to arrive home at about 1:30am on Saturday, according to a foreign ministry release issued on Friday....Local business directory

item.title: Govt prioritises safety of Bangladeshis in ME, says state minister
item.description: Bangladesh state minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Friday reaffirmed that the government is working tirelessly to ensure the safety and security of Bangladeshis living in the Middle East amid the ongoing tensions....Bangladeshi culture blog

item.title: NSU Alumni Association hosts Ramadan gathering in Dhaka
item.description: The North South University Alumni Association (NSUAA) hosted a Ramadan gathering, bringing together alumni to celebrate the spirit of togetherness, reflection and blessings of the holy month, said a press release....

item.title: Norway crown princess says ‘manipulated’ by Epstein
item.description: Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit said she was ‘manipulated’ by convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, in an interview broadcast Friday in which she sought to explain their close relationship....

item.title: Israeli police attack Muslims, prevent Eid prayers at Al-Aqsa Mosque
item.description: Israeli police used force to prevent Muslim devotees from entering the Al-Aqsa Mosque, Islam’s third holiest site in Jerusalem’s Old City, on Friday morning as they attempted to offer Eid-ul-Fitr prayers......

item.title: Muslims hold Eid prayers outside as Israel seals Al-Aqsa mosque
item.description: Hundreds of Muslim worshippers held Eid prayers at the gates of Jerusalem’s Old City Friday, with Israel closing access to the Al-Aqsa mosque and other holy sites over the war with Iran....

item.title: Running app reveals location of France aircraft carrier
item.description: A member of the French navy using an app to track his jogging performance broadcast the exact position of his country’s flagship aircraft carrier, a newspaper has reported....

item.title: Eid Jamaat to be held at JS South Plaza
item.description: A congregation for holy Eid-ul-Fitr will be held at 8:00am at the South Plaza of the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban....

item.title: Main Eid Jamaat at 8:30am at National Eidgah, five at Baitul Mukarram Mosque
item.description: Bangladesh’s main Eid Jamaat will be held at the National Eidgah at 8:30am on Saturday on the occasion of the holy Eid-ul-Fitr....Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Sholakia ready for Eid congregation with four-tier security
item.description: Bangladesh’s largest Eid congregation ground, the historic Sholakia Eidgah in Kishoreganj, is fully prepared for the 199th Eid-ul-Fitr congregation....

item.title: Jumatul Wida observed in Bangladesh
item.description: The Jumatul Wida, the last Friday of the holy month of Ramadan, was observed across Bangladesh on Friday with due solemnity and religious fervour....

item.title: Jumatul Wida observed in Bangladesh
item.description: The Jumatul Wida, the last Friday of the holy month of Ramadan, was observed across Bangladesh on Friday with due solemnity and religious fervour....

item.title: Sri Lanka denied US request to station warplanes
item.description: Sri Lanka refused permission to the United States to station two of its warplanes at a civilian airport in the island’s south in early March, President Anura Kumara Dissanayake said on Friday....
